filter_rules: Drop rows whose word field contains Chinese characters or non-letter symbols.
Drop multi-word phrases, spaced expressions, hyphenated forms, and similar non-single-word entries by keeping only A-Z tokens.
Drop capitalized proper nouns, place names, brands, myth/history names, and acronym-like entries.
Drop only a very small set of obvious misspellings, corrupted tokens, or highly idiosyncratic non-general items.

assessment.cefr_estimate: B2+ overall, with reading ability touching C1 in topics you care about
assessment.summary: Your unknown-word list mixes some everyday gaps with a large amount of upper-B2/C1 abstract vocabulary plus specialized technical terms. That usually suggests a strong B2 learner with uneven but ambitious reading exposure, especially in analytic or curiosity-driven domains.
assessment.profile_shape: interest-driven and spiky rather than uniformly general-purpose

agent_guidance.default_input_difficulty: Use upper-B2 / lower-C1 English by default.
agent_guidance.preserve_domain_terms: Do not simplify away math, finance, technical, or academic vocabulary when it is relevant to the topic.
agent_guidance.gloss_when: Gloss dense idioms, phrasal verbs, or stacked specialist terms only when they are likely to block comprehension.
agent_guidance.good_next_band: High-frequency C1 abstract vocabulary, academic collocations, nuanced phrasal verbs, plus domain reading in math/finance/technology.
agent_guidance.avoid_by_default: Avoid unnecessary cultural proper nouns and niche reference-heavy jargon unless the task specifically needs them.

assessment.caveat: This is still an inference from saved unknown-word lists rather than a controlled vocabulary test, so other agents should use it as calibration guidance, not as a precise score.
